name: Connected consumer thermostat
description: A Wi-Fi connected thermostat for residential heating/cooling control, with cloud connectivity, mobile app interface, local display and controls, and OTA firmware updates. Typical examples: Nest, Ecobee, Tado, Honeywell Home T6. Subject to EN 303 645, EN 18031-1 and EN 18031-2 (mandatory under RED from August 2025), and CRA Annex I.

component_templates.name: Cloud backend service
elicitation_questions: Does the thermostat connect to a manufacturer cloud service or backend?
What protocol is used for cloud communication? (e.g. MQTT, HTTPS, WebSocket, proprietary)
Is the cloud connection always-on or on-demand?
Is the cloud connection required for basic thermostat operation (heating/cooling control), or does the device work locally without it?

component_templates.name: Mobile companion app
elicitation_questions: Does the product include a companion mobile app (iOS and/or Android)?
Does the app communicate with the thermostat directly (local Wi-Fi / Bluetooth) or only through the cloud?
Is the app required for initial setup / onboarding?
Does the app require account authentication to access thermostat controls?

component_templates.name: Local display and user interface
elicitation_questions: Does the thermostat have a local display (LCD, e-ink, LED) showing temperature and status?
Does the device have physical controls? (touch buttons, capacitive touch, rotary dial)
Can the local UI access all configuration settings, or is it limited to basic controls (setpoint, mode)?
Is authentication required to access any local settings? (e.g. installer PIN, access code)

component_templates.name: Wi-Fi interface
elicitation_questions: Which Wi-Fi standards does the device support? (802.11 b/g/n/ac/ax, 2.4GHz only or dual-band)
What Wi-Fi security protocols are supported? (WPA2, WPA3)
Does the device expose any network services or open ports beyond what is needed for its cloud connection? (e.g. UPnP, mDNS, HTTP server)
Is there a local API accessible over Wi-Fi? (e.g. REST API for home automation integration)

component_templates.name: Firmware and OTA update agent
elicitation_questions: Does the device support over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates?
Are firmware updates delivered automatically, or does the user need to approve them?
Are firmware images digitally signed and is the signature verified before installation?
Is there rollback protection to prevent downgrading to a vulnerable firmware version?
What is the published support period for this product? (months or end-date)
Does the device display a notification to the user when an update is available or has been applied?

component_templates.name: Secure credential and key store
elicitation_questions: Where are Wi-Fi credentials, cloud authentication tokens, and TLS certificates stored on the device?
Are credentials stored in plaintext in flash, or is there hardware-backed secure storage? (TEE, secure element, encrypted partition)
Are there any hard-coded credentials or API keys embedded in the firmware?
Are per-device unique keys used for cloud authentication, or is a shared secret used across all units of the same model?

component_templates.name: Local scheduling and control logic
elicitation_questions: Does the thermostat operate independently when the cloud connection is unavailable? (local schedule execution, manual setpoint)
How does the device behave after a power outage — does it recover to its last schedule automatically?
Is there a fail-safe temperature: does the device maintain a minimum/maximum temperature even if all connectivity is lost?
Does the device validate commands received from the cloud or app before applying them to HVAC outputs?

component_templates.name: Heating/cooling equipment relay or output
elicitation_questions: How does the thermostat control the HVAC system — direct relay switching (24V wiring), or a proprietary smart HVAC protocol?
Is the HVAC control interface bi-directional (thermostat receives status from HVAC) or output-only?
Is there any safety interlock preventing unreasonable commands being sent to HVAC equipment? (e.g. simultaneous heating and cooling inhibit)

component_templates.name: Temperature and environmental sensors
elicitation_questions: What sensors does the device include? (temperature, humidity, occupancy/PIR, CO2, air quality, proximity)
Is sensor data sent to the cloud, or only used locally?
Is sensor data used for any purposes beyond HVAC control? (e.g. occupancy analytics, energy reporting sent to third parties)
Are occupancy or presence sensing capabilities documented and disclosed to the user?

component_templates.name: Debug and hardware interfaces
elicitation_questions: Does the PCB expose any debug interfaces accessible without opening the enclosure? (external UART, test pads on case edge)
Are JTAG/SWD/UART debug ports present internally? Are they disabled in production firmware?
Is secure boot enabled — is the bootloader locked against loading unsigned firmware?

component_templates.name: Local data store
elicitation_questions: What data does the thermostat store persistently on-device? (schedules, usage history, credentials, logs)
Is any personal data stored locally (e.g. occupancy patterns, names associated with schedules)?
Does the device provide a factory reset function that durably erases all user data including credentials and schedules?
Is the factory reset accessible from the local UI without requiring cloud connectivity?

component_templates.name: Bluetooth / BLE interface
elicitation_questions: Does the device include Bluetooth or BLE?
What is BLE used for? (initial setup / provisioning only, ongoing remote control, proximity detection)
Is BLE disabled after provisioning is complete, or does it remain active during normal operation?
Is BLE communication authenticated and encrypted?

component_templates.name: Telemetry and logging service
elicitation_questions: Does the thermostat send telemetry or usage data to the manufacturer? (energy consumption, HVAC run times, error logs, feature usage)
Is telemetry data sent to any third parties (analytics platforms, energy providers)?
Can the user opt out of telemetry collection?
Is telemetry data anonymised or pseudonymised before transmission?
Is the cloud connection encrypted end-to-end with TLS? Which version (1.2, 1.3)?
Is the server certificate validated by the device, or is certificate validation disabled?
Can the cloud service push commands to the thermostat (e.g. setpoint changes, firmware triggers) without user confirmation on the device?
Does the mobile app authenticate to the cloud with per-user credentials (username/password, OAuth token)?
Is multi-factor authentication available or required for app login?
Can multiple user accounts access the same thermostat, and is there access control between accounts (owner vs guest)?
Are firmware update packages delivered from the same cloud endpoint as regular control traffic, or a separate update server?
Can the cloud trigger a firmware update automatically without user consent, or is user approval required?
Is there a mechanism for the device to verify it is downloading the update from a legitimate source and not a spoofed server?
Is the HVAC control wiring standard 24V low-voltage (R, C, W, Y, G terminals) or a digital bus?
Is the heating output physically protected against unexpected activation? (e.g. hardware interlock, watchdog timer)
Is raw sensor data (including occupancy, humidity) included in telemetry sent to the cloud, or only processed aggregates?
How long is sensor data retained on the device before being overwritten or deleted?
Is telemetry transmitted over the same encrypted channel as control traffic?
Are third-party analytics services (e.g. Firebase, Datadog, Amplitude) embedded in the device firmware or app and receiving telemetry data?
Does the manufacturer's privacy policy clearly state what telemetry data is collected, for how long, and with whom it is shared?
Does the BLE interface expose any commands that can modify thermostat configuration without requiring cloud authentication?
Is BLE communication protected with BLE pairing (Just Works, Passkey, Numeric Comparison) or a higher-level application layer auth?
Is the BLE interface disabled or put into non-discoverable mode after provisioning?
Does the device expose a local REST API or other local interface over Wi-Fi for home automation integration? (e.g. for Home Assistant, Apple HomeKit, Matter)
If a local API exists, is it authenticated? Can any device on the home network send commands to the thermostat?
Does the device respond to mDNS or UPnP discovery that could expose device information to any host on the network?

threat_hints.title: Spoofed cloud MQTT broker
threat_hints.description: An attacker intercepts or redirects the device's MQTT connection to a rogue broker, injecting malicious setpoint commands or exfiltrating credentials.
threat_hints.mitigation: Mutual TLS with pinned server certificate; validate broker hostname against hardcoded allowlist

threat_hints.title: Unsigned firmware accepted over OTA
threat_hints.description: A malicious firmware image is accepted if signature verification is absent, bypassable, or uses a hardcoded shared key common to all units.
threat_hints.mitigation: Per-device or model-specific firmware signing; hardware-rooted verification at boot; anti-rollback via version counter in secure storage

threat_hints.title: Wi-Fi credentials extracted from flash
threat_hints.description: An attacker with physical access to the PCB reads unencrypted Wi-Fi credentials from flash memory via JTAG or direct flash dump, gaining access to the home network.
threat_hints.mitigation: Encrypt credential storage with hardware-backed key (TEE, secure element); disable debug interfaces in production

threat_hints.title: Debug interface enables firmware injection
threat_hints.description: An attacker with brief physical access uses an exposed JTAG or UART debug port to extract firmware, read secrets, or inject a modified bootloader.
threat_hints.mitigation: Permanently disable JTAG/SWD in production fuses; enable secure boot with hardware root of trust; no exposed debug test pads on external PCB edges

threat_hints.title: Unauthenticated BLE command injection
threat_hints.description: BLE GATT characteristics for thermostat control or provisioning remain accessible after setup, allowing any nearby device to change setpoints or extract credentials without authentication.
threat_hints.mitigation: Disable BLE after provisioning; require BLE-level pairing + application-layer auth token for any GATT write; set device to non-discoverable after setup

threat_hints.title: Unauthenticated local API on home network
threat_hints.description: A local HTTP/REST API exposed on the home LAN allows any device on the network to change thermostat settings without authentication, enabling a compromised device to pivot to HVAC control.
threat_hints.mitigation: Require authentication token for all local API calls; limit local API surface to read-only or require confirmation on device display for writes

threat_hints.title: Occupancy data disclosure to third parties
threat_hints.description: Occupancy or presence sensor data is transmitted to third-party analytics services embedded in the firmware without adequate user notice or consent, violating GDPR and EN 303 645 data provisions.
threat_hints.mitigation: Explicit disclosure of all sensing capabilities and data recipients; opt-in consent for non-essential data collection; data minimisation — aggregate only, no raw occupancy timestamps to cloud

threat_hints.title: Cloud-dependent failure — thermostat inoperable without internet
threat_hints.description: The thermostat fails to maintain heating/cooling schedules when the cloud connection is unavailable, leaving occupants without climate control during outages.
threat_hints.mitigation: Onboard schedule execution must be independent of cloud connectivity; fail-safe minimum temperature maintained even without network; graceful reconnection with exponential back-off

threat_hints.title: Universal default password on web or local interface
threat_hints.description: The device ships with a predictable or universal default credential for the local UI or cloud account, enabling trivial takeover by anyone who knows the model default.
threat_hints.mitigation: Unique per-device credentials generated at manufacture; force credential change on first login; no shared default passwords across product class

threat_hints.title: Personal data not erased on factory reset
threat_hints.description: Factory reset fails to durably erase schedule data, occupancy logs, or Wi-Fi credentials from flash, exposing previous owner's data when a device is resold or returned.
threat_hints.mitigation: Factory reset must cryptographically erase or overwrite all user data partitions, including credentials, schedules and logs; accessible from local UI without cloud connectivity

threat_hints.title: Malicious setpoint injection via compromised cloud
threat_hints.description: A compromised cloud account or server-side vulnerability allows an attacker to push extreme setpoints to the thermostat, causing discomfort, property damage (frozen pipes), or excess energy consumption.
threat_hints.mitigation: Device-side validation of commanded setpoints within safe physical bounds; rate-limiting of remote setpoint changes; local override always available without network

threat_hints.title: No vulnerability disclosure policy
threat_hints.description: The manufacturer has no publicly accessible CVD policy or contact address for reporting security issues, violating EN 303 645 provision 5.2-1 and CRA Part II obligations.
threat_hints.mitigation: Publish CVD policy at a stable URL (e.g. security.txt, manufacturer website); include contact email, acknowledgement timelines, and status update commitments
